plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero for capital preservation.
Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 15% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Exit SMH due to its downtrend and trim flag.
Initiate a new position in ZS at 8% due to its strong scanner rating and quality.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, with a realistic ~0.5-1.5% monthly outlook. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by cutting leverage and reducing concentration. Expect lower drawdowns but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Sell SOXL and SPAL to zero to remove high-beta leveraged exposure.
Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ to 10% each, addressing their downtrend and trim flags.
Exit SMH due to its downtrend and trim flag.
Initiate a new position in ZS at 10% for quality tech growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan prioritizes risk-first growth, targeting ~1.5-2.5% monthly. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index and quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns with a focus on sustainable growth.

plan: Sell SOXL and SPAL to zero as they are below stop and reallocate capital.
Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandates.
Exit QQQI, QQQ, and SMH due to downtrends and trim flags to reallocate to stronger setups.
Initiate a significant position in ZS at 15% due to its top scanner score and strong risk/reward.
Add new positions in NVDA at 10% and ASML at 10% leveraging their strong scanner ratings.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets ~3.0-4.0% monthly by concentrating in high-scoring setups. It involves higher leverage and concentration, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of potential rapid losses and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero to eliminate high-beta exposure.
Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Significantly increase QQQI to 30% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 10% and CRWD to 5% to lower overall growth-oriented exposure.
Sell SMH due to its downtrend and high-beta nature, focusing on low drawdown.
income.read: This income-focused plan aims for ~0.5-1.0% monthly, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta names, rotating into income-generating ETFs. Distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in a selloff; this is not a savings account.
